message:
perf(btree+update): in-place same-size UPDATE fast path

Adds a narrow btree primitive that overwrites the payload bytes of the
currently positioned leaf-table row when the new record matches the old
record in local size and the cell does not spill into overflow pages.
This avoids the delete + reseek + reinsert sequence for the common case
of UPDATE statements that change column values without changing the
serialized record length (e.g. fixed-width integer/blob updates).

Changes:

- crates/fsqlite-btree/src/cursor.rs:
  - New `BtCursor::table_overwrite_current_payload_same_size_no_overflow`
    public-but-hidden primitive. Verifies the cursor sits on a leaf-table
    page, the targeted rowid matches, the cell has no overflow, and the
    new payload's length equals both `payload_size` and `local_size`
    before patching the page bytes in place. Preserves cell header,
    rowid varint, pointer array, freeblock chain, and cursor position;
    callers fall back to delete+insert on any mismatch.
  - Bumps the row-image epoch on success so dependent caches invalidate.
  - Bounds-checks the patched range against both the page buffer and the
    declared usable size, returning DatabaseCorrupt on overflow rather
    than silently writing past the end.
  - Adds a unit test that exercises the success path, the size-change
    rejection (must return Ok(false) and leave bytes untouched), and the
    surrounding row ordering after the rewrite.

- crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs:
  - Direct UPDATE path now tries the same-size overwrite first and only
    falls through to the existing delete + table_insert_prechecked_absent
    sequence when the cursor reports the fast path is not applicable.
    Behavior is unchanged for size-changing or overflow-spilling rows.
